FindingDescription: State 49 shows a provider fraud rate of 19.6% — 2.1x the national average of 9.4%. Average provider reimbursement in this state is 1.2x the national median. 107 providers analysed. What is driving this geographic concentration of fraud?

GroundTruth.interpretation: State fraud rates differ significantly (chi2=72.7, p=0.0247). Worst state vs rest: h=0.303 (small effect). Cramér's V=0.116. Geographic concentration is WEAK.

GTInterpretation: State fraud rates differ significantly (chi2=72.7, p=0.0247). Worst state vs rest: h=0.303 (small effect). Cramér's V=0.116. Geographic concentration is WEAK.

FindingDescription: Q2 shows a claim-level fraud rate of 38.2% — 1.0x higher than Q4 (38.0%). Average reimbursement in the peak quarter is $993 vs $1,015 in the low quarter. Is there a seasonal pattern in fraudulent billing behaviour?

GroundTruth.interpretation: Quarterly fraud rates: [np.float64(0.381), np.float64(0.382), np.float64(0.382), np.float64(0.38)]. ANOVA F=0.74, p=0.5290, eta²=0.0000. Seasonal effect is SPURIOUS.

GTInterpretation: Quarterly fraud rates: [np.float64(0.381), np.float64(0.382), np.float64(0.382), np.float64(0.38)]. ANOVA F=0.74, p=0.5290, eta²=0.0000. Seasonal effect is SPURIOUS.

FindingDescription: Providers with 11-50 unique attending physicians show a fraud rate of 14.0% — 9.3x higher than single-physician providers (1.5%). Does physician network size predict organised fraud rings where multiple physicians share billing across providers?

GroundTruth.interpretation: High-physician providers fraud rate: 0.174 vs single-physician: 0.015. t=17.41, p=0.0000, d=0.496 (small). Physician network effect is WEAK.

GTInterpretation: High-physician providers fraud rate: 0.174 vs single-physician: 0.015. t=17.41, p=0.0000, d=0.496 (small). Physician network effect is WEAK.

FindingDescription: Outpatient claim reimbursements show a bimodal distribution: 68.3% of claims cluster below $200 and 7.0% exceed $1,000. The high-value cluster (>$1,000) has a fraud rate of 36.5% vs 36.6% in the low cluster. What explains this bimodal separation and why does the high-value cluster have elevated fraud association?

GroundTruth.interpretation: High cluster (>$1000) fraud rate: 0.365 vs low cluster (<$200): 0.366. t=-0.56, p=0.5771, d=-0.003 (negligible). Bimodal fraud difference is SPURIOUS.

GTInterpretation: High cluster (>$1000) fraud rate: 0.365 vs low cluster (<$200): 0.366. t=-0.56, p=0.5771, d=-0.003 (negligible). Bimodal fraud difference is SPURIOUS.

FindingDescription: Fraud-flagged provider claims are submitted for patients with an average of 17.46 chronic conditions, compared to 17.53 for non-fraud providers (p=0.0000, t-test). 0.0% of fraud claims involve patients with zero recorded chronic conditions. Are fraud providers systematically billing expensive procedures for patients whose medical profiles do not justify them?
ComparisonBaseline: Non-fraud provider patient chronic condition avg

GroundTruth.interpretation: Fraud claims avg chronic conditions: 17.458 vs non-fraud: 17.528. t=-10.97, p=0.0000, d=-0.030 (negligible). Chronic condition mismatch is WEAK.

GTInterpretation: Fraud claims avg chronic conditions: 17.458 vs non-fraud: 17.528. t=-10.97, p=0.0000, d=-0.030 (negligible). Chronic condition mismatch is WEAK.

FindingDescription: 12.8% of inpatient claims have admission duration of 0-1 days yet average reimbursement of $7,166 — compared to $10,518 for longer stays. Short-stay claims have a fraud rate of 59.2% vs 57.6% for longer stays. Are providers billing inpatient-level reimbursements for effectively outpatient procedures?

GroundTruth.interpretation: Short-stay (0-1d) fraud rate: 0.592 vs long-stay: 0.576. t=2.20, p=0.0281, d=0.032 (negligible). Reimbursement diff: short=$7,166 vs long=$10,518 (d=-0.327). Short admission anomaly is WEAK.

GTInterpretation: Short-stay (0-1d) fraud rate: 0.592 vs long-stay: 0.576. t=2.20, p=0.0281, d=0.032 (negligible). Reimbursement diff: short=$7,166 vs long=$10,518 (d=-0.327). Short admission anomaly is WEAK.

FindingDescription: Providers in the top 5% of reimbursement vs state peer median bill on average 53.5x their state peers and show a fraud rate of 72.3% — compared to 6.0% for providers billing within peer norms. 271 providers identified. Does extreme peer group deviation systematically predict fraud beyond what absolute reimbursement levels reveal?

GroundTruth.interpretation: Top-5% peer deviants fraud rate: 0.723 vs normal providers: 0.060. U=1157952, p=0.0000, r=-0.663. Peer deviation fraud signal is CONFIRMED.

GTInterpretation: Top-5% peer deviants fraud rate: 0.723 vs normal providers: 0.060. U=1157952, p=0.0000, r=-0.663. Peer deviation fraud signal is CONFIRMED.

FindingDescription: The top 5 diagnosis codes by fraud rate show an average fraud association of 63.9% — 1.7x the overall rate of 38.1%. These codes (including 44024, 55221) average $12,830 per claim. Are specific diagnosis codes being systematically exploited to justify high-reimbursement claims across fraud providers?

GroundTruth.interpretation: Top-5 fraud diagnosis codes fraud rate: 0.639 vs rest: 0.381. chi2=119.6, p=0.0000, V=0.015. Diagnosis code concentration is WEAK.

GTInterpretation: Top-5 fraud diagnosis codes fraud rate: 0.639 vs rest: 0.381. chi2=119.6, p=0.0000, V=0.015. Diagnosis code concentration is WEAK.

FindingDescription: 81.3% of all claims have a duration of 0 days (ClaimStartDt = ClaimEndDt) yet average reimbursement of $241. These zero-duration claims have a fraud rate of 36.6% vs 44.9% for claims with non-zero duration. Are zero-duration claims being used to submit high-value reimbursements for services that required no extended care?

GroundTruth.interpretation: Zero-duration fraud rate: 0.366 vs non-zero: 0.449. t=-48.89, p=0.0000, d=-0.171 (negligible). Zero-duration anomaly is WEAK.

GTInterpretation: Zero-duration fraud rate: 0.366 vs non-zero: 0.449. t=-48.89, p=0.0000, d=-0.171 (negligible). Zero-duration anomaly is WEAK.

FindingDescription: 3,837 beneficiaries (2.77% of all patients) are treated by 3 or more providers that are ALL fraud-flagged. These patients have an average total reimbursement of $7,359 across their fraud provider visits. Are certain patients being used as shared billing vehicles across a coordinated fraud provider network?

GroundTruth.interpretation: Multi-provider patients (3+) are 0.49x more likely to be exclusively at fraud providers. p=1.0000, log_OR=-0.710. Multi-provider fraud pattern is SPURIOUS.

GTInterpretation: Multi-provider patients (3+) are 0.49x more likely to be exclusively at fraud providers. p=1.0000, log_OR=-0.710. Multi-provider fraud pattern is SPURIOUS.
